title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Professor Yardeley suggests that investigators follow bloodstains on a suspect's clothing, but Ellery argues that the killer, Croscot, likely planned the crime and prepared a change of clothes to avoid detection. Ellery predicts the suspect will be a man with a limp carrying a small suitcase or package. In response, Detective Wain orders patrol boats to search for Croscot.

The group moves to Captain Swift's cabin, where he is found with a deep head wound. Dr. Temple treats the Captain's injury with bandages. Ellery notices a short-barreled semi-automatic pistol on a table near the Captain's bunk, though Vance notes the weapon has not been fired. When questioned by District Attorney Eisman and others, Captain Swift explains that he and Megalos rowed back to the *Helen* the previous night. Swift reports that he went to sleep at "seven bells" (11:30 PM) after walking the deck, but woke up feeling something was wrong and hearing heavy breathing beside his bunk. Before he could reach for his gun, a flash of light blinded him and he was struck on the head.

Dr. Rumson examines Megalos's body and confirms his identity via a hernia, estimating the time of death occurred between 1:00 and 1:30 AM. No items were stolen from the cabin or the safe. Expressing professional despair and frustration at being unable to grasp certain details, Ellery suggests that authorities prepare trawling equipment to search for Megalos's missing head.
